title: Introduction
description: NOC 60020 – Retail and Wholesale Trade Managers | GTR Immigration NOC 60020 captures the leadership of Canada’s massive retail and wholesale sectors. Trade managers are responsible for the operational success of everything from large-scale distribution centers to high-end retail chains. As supply chain management becomes increasingly complex, the need for experienced international trade managers is at an all-time high. GTR Immigration specializes in helping retail leaders translate their operational expertise into a Canadian PR strategy.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level0Major Group60 – Executive and senior management occupationsSub-Major Group600 – Management occupationsMinor Group6002 – Managers in retail and wholesale tradeNOC Code60020

duties: Plan, direct, and evaluate the daily operations of retail or wholesale establishments.
Manage staff and assign duties to employees.
Study market research and trends to determine consumer demand and inventory levels.
Develop and implement marketing and pricing strategies.
Resolve customer complaints and oversee the recruitment and training of staff.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A university degree or college diploma in business administration or a related field is often required.
Several years of experience in retail or wholesale trade, including supervisory experience, are mandatory.
job_outlook: The outlook is Moderate to Good. The continuous demand for consumer goods and the modernization of wholesale networks will drive job creation in this sector over the next ten years.
labour_market_demand: The shift toward e-commerce and omnichannel retail has revolutionized this sector. Demand is high for managers who can oversee complex inventory systems and large-scale logistics. Both rural areas needing wholesale managers and urban centers needing retail leaders offer significant job opportunities.
Massive Sector: Retail is one of Canada's largest employers.
Technological Integration: Lead digital transformations in traditional trade environments.
Stability: Essential nature of the wholesale and retail sectors ensures long-term job security.
pathways: Express Entry (CEC): Ideal for managers with one year of Canadian work experience.
Provincial Nominee Program (PNP): Specifically targeting retail managers in provinces like Saskatchewan and Alberta.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): For trade managers moving to the Atlantic provinces.
